methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Galadyne, a pre-seed stage Defense & Security company founded by Chandler Luzsicza, has raised $4.8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 10 private and 2 public peers in the Defense & Security sector, including companies such as Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

Only one valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. The Stage-Progression method, which applies a typical pre-seed to next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x to the $4.8M raised proxy, yields an implied valuation of $12M. A peer-median approach was not feasible due to insufficient comparable data—while 20 same-sector peers exist, filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier produced fewer than three datapoints, making cross-sectional benchmarking unreliable.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $10M (low) to $14M (high), with a median of $12M. This range is anchored entirely to stage-progression mechanics rather than market multiples. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow peer comps at the pre-seed stage in this sector, the early revenue profile of the target, and execution risk typical of the stage. Any material change in capital efficiency or product-market signals would warrant re-anchoring.
